mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods of PAYEMS growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts (HOUST) reflects increased consumer confidence and demand for durable goods, which correlates with higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods of HOUST growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 220 (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in CPIAUCSL > 220 periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal accommodative monetary policy and disinflation, creating favorable conditions for equity market gains over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods of FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL declines are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates monetary tightening, which raises borrowing costs and reduces corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in FEDFUNDS > 4.5% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in INDPRO growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) signals stronger aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in GDPC1 growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.05.
